steps.explanation: In the function \(f(x) = 3{,}000(0.75)^x\), the variable \(x\) is years after 2008. Setting \(x = 0\) gives the population at the starting year 2008: \(f(0) = 3{,}000 \cdot (0.75)^0 = 3{,}000 \cdot 1 = 3{,}000\).
In any exponential model \(f(x) = a \cdot b^x\), the coefficient \(a\) is the initial value when \(x = 0\). Here \(a = 3{,}000\) is the population at \(x = 0\), which corresponds to 2008.

Evaluate each choice:
  • Choice A: “estimated percent decrease every 8 years after 2008.” The number 3,000 is not a percent, and \(x\) counts individual years, not 8-year blocks. Incorrect.
  • Choice B: “estimated percent decrease each year after 2008.” The annual decay factor is 0.75, not 3,000; 3,000 is not a percent. Incorrect.
  • Choice C: “estimated population 8 years after 2008.” \(f(8) = 3{,}000(0.75)^8 \approx 300\), not 3,000. Incorrect.
  • Choice D: “estimated initial population in 2008.” \(f(0) = 3{,}000\), so 3,000 is the population when the program began in 2008. That matches choice D — D is correct.
